course.title: Modern JavaScript: Runtime to Accessible Application
course.description: Learners moving from HTML and CSS into rigorous modern JavaScript application development. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate ecmascript 2026 fundamentals and runtime semantics through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate asynchronous javascript, modules, dom, accessible interaction, web data, security, testing, and release through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate four cumulative stakeholder projects through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Distinguish ECMAScript language behavior from browser, worker, and server host APIs.
misconceptions: JavaScript and the browser are the same runtime specification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish ECMAScript language behavior from browser, worker, and server host APIs.

competencies.statement: Trace source parsing, module instantiation, execution contexts, jobs, and observable output.
misconceptions: JavaScript executes every line immediately from top to bottom without setup.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace source parsing, module instantiation, execution contexts, jobs, and observable output.

competencies.statement: Use console evidence, breakpoints, scope inspection, watch expressions, and stack traces without relying on logging alone.
misconceptions: Adding more console logs always reveals the root cause.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use console evidence, breakpoints, scope inspection, watch expressions, and stack traces without relying on logging alone.

competencies.statement: Explain strict-mode behavior and use modules without depending on error-prone sloppy-mode semantics.
misconceptions: Strict mode only changes formatting and performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain strict-mode behavior and use modules without depending on error-prone sloppy-mode semantics.

competencies.statement: Relate transformed output to authored source with source maps while verifying the actual shipped artifact.
misconceptions: A source map proves the deployed bundle matches local source.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate transformed output to authored source with source maps while verifying the actual shipped artifact.

competencies.statement: Record runtime, module mode, inputs, locale, time, and dependency conditions needed to reproduce behavior.
misconceptions: The same script behaves identically in every JavaScript host.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record runtime, module mode, inputs, locale, time, and dependency conditions needed to reproduce behavior.

competencies.statement: Distinguish undefined, null, Boolean, Number, BigInt, String, Symbol, and object values by behavior and use.
misconceptions: Null and undefined are interchangeable empty strings.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish undefined, null, Boolean, Number, BigInt, String, Symbol, and object values by behavior and use.

competencies.statement: Use typeof, Array.isArray, instanceof, and explicit predicates while accounting for their boundaries.
misconceptions: Typeof precisely identifies every JavaScript value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use typeof, Array.isArray, instanceof, and explicit predicates while accounting for their boundaries.

competencies.statement: Predict implicit coercion and prefer explicit conversion at external data boundaries.
misconceptions: The plus operator always performs numeric addition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict implicit coercion and prefer explicit conversion at external data boundaries.

competencies.statement: Choose strict equality, Object.is, or collection sameness based on NaN, signed zero, identity, and domain needs.
misconceptions: Double and triple equals differ only in coding style.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose strict equality, Object.is, or collection sameness based on NaN, signed zero, identity, and domain needs.

competencies.statement: Handle IEEE-754 precision, NaN, Infinity, safe integers, parsing, rounding, and decimal-sensitive decisions.
misconceptions: Every integer and decimal is represented exactly as a Number.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle IEEE-754 precision, NaN, Infinity, safe integers, parsing, rounding, and decimal-sensitive decisions.

competencies.statement: Use BigInt and Symbol only where their arithmetic, serialization, identity, and interoperability limits fit.
misconceptions: BigInt can mix directly with Number in any arithmetic expression.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use BigInt and Symbol only where their arithmetic, serialization, identity, and interoperability limits fit.

competencies.statement: Choose const, let, or legacy var from reassignment, scope, and initialization behavior.
misconceptions: Const makes the referenced object deeply immutable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose const, let, or legacy var from reassignment, scope, and initialization behavior.

competencies.statement: Resolve names through lexical environments and identify shadowing without guessing from call order.
misconceptions: A function reads variables from wherever it is called.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve names through lexical environments and identify shadowing without guessing from call order.

competencies.statement: Predict declaration instantiation, function hoisting, and temporal dead-zone failures.
misconceptions: Let and const are not hoisted in any sense.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict declaration instantiation, function hoisting, and temporal dead-zone failures.

competencies.statement: Use closures to retain purposeful private state while recognizing capture and lifetime costs.
misconceptions: A closure copies each captured value at function creation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use closures to retain purposeful private state while recognizing capture and lifetime costs.

competencies.statement: Preserve per-iteration bindings in callbacks and asynchronous work without index drift.
misconceptions: Every loop callback automatically receives a frozen index.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve per-iteration bindings in callbacks and asynchronous work without index drift.

competencies.statement: Reason about reachability, event-listener cleanup, weak references, and accidental retention without manual memory control.
misconceptions: The garbage collector removes every object as soon as a function returns.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reason about reachability, event-listener cleanup, weak references, and accidental retention without manual memory control.

competencies.statement: Predict expression grouping, short-circuit evaluation, assignment, optional chaining, and nullish coalescing.
misconceptions: Nullish coalescing and logical OR reject the same values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict expression grouping, short-circuit evaluation, assignment, optional chaining, and nullish coalescing.

competencies.statement: Use truthiness only when every falsy value shares the same domain meaning and otherwise write explicit guards.
misconceptions: A falsy value always means missing data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use truthiness only when every falsy value shares the same domain meaning and otherwise write explicit guards.

competencies.statement: Express mutually exclusive branches, guard clauses, and exhaustive fallbacks without unreachable paths.
misconceptions: Nested conditionals are required for every multi-rule decision.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Express mutually exclusive branches, guard clauses, and exhaustive fallbacks without unreachable paths.

competencies.statement: Use switch matching, scoped cases, fallthrough, and default behavior deliberately.
misconceptions: Every switch case stops automatically after its statements.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use switch matching, scoped cases, fallthrough, and default behavior deliberately.

competencies.statement: Choose for, while, for-of, or collection operations from iteration meaning, mutation, and termination needs.
misconceptions: For-in is the standard way to iterate array values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose for, while, for-of, or collection operations from iteration meaning, mutation, and termination needs.

competencies.statement: Use break, continue, labels, and early return without skipping cleanup or required records.
misconceptions: A continue statement always advances every loop state variable safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use break, continue, labels, and early return without skipping cleanup or required records.

competencies.statement: Choose function declarations, expressions, arrows, and methods from hoisting, this, construction, and readability needs.
misconceptions: Arrow functions are shorter replacements for every function form.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose function declarations, expressions, arrows, and methods from hoisting, this, construction, and readability needs.

competencies.statement: Use defaults, rest parameters, destructuring, and validation without hiding required inputs.
misconceptions: Default parameters run only when an argument is null.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use defaults, rest parameters, destructuring, and validation without hiding required inputs.

competencies.statement: Separate returned values, mutations, I/O, and thrown failures into an understandable callable contract.
misconceptions: A function that logs a value also returns that logged value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate returned values, mutations, I/O, and thrown failures into an understandable callable contract.

competencies.statement: Predict this from call form and use bind, call, apply, or lexical capture only when the ownership model requires it.
misconceptions: This refers to the function where it was written.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict this from call form and use bind, call, apply, or lexical capture only when the ownership model requires it.

competencies.statement: Design callbacks and higher-order functions with explicit timing, error, and return-value contracts.
misconceptions: Passing a function always executes it immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design callbacks and higher-order functions with explicit timing, error, and return-value contracts.

competencies.statement: Choose recursion or iteration from data shape, stack depth, termination, and clarity constraints.
misconceptions: Tail-recursive JavaScript is guaranteed to use constant stack space in all engines.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose recursion or iteration from data shape, stack depth, termination, and clarity constraints.

competencies.statement: Choose mutating or copying array operations and document ownership before updating shared data.
misconceptions: Every array method returns a new independent array.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose mutating or copying array operations and document ownership before updating shared data.

competencies.statement: Use map, filter, reduce, find, some, every, flat, and flatMap from the required output and early-exit behavior.
misconceptions: Reduce is always clearer and faster than named intermediate operations.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use map, filter, reduce, find, some, every, flat, and flatMap from the required output and early-exit behavior.

competencies.statement: Sort with a valid comparator, copying policy, stability expectations, and locale-aware text rules.
misconceptions: Default array sorting orders numbers numerically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Sort with a valid comparator, copying policy, stability expectations, and locale-aware text rules.

competencies.statement: Choose Map, Set, WeakMap, or WeakSet from key identity, enumeration, retention, and lookup needs.
misconceptions: Map and plain object keys have identical behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose Map, Set, WeakMap, or WeakSet from key identity, enumeration, retention, and lookup needs.

competencies.statement: Implement and consume iterable and iterator protocols with correct done and value transitions.
misconceptions: Any object with a length property is automatically iterable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement and consume iterable and iterator protocols with correct done and value transitions.

competencies.statement: Use generators for lazy staged iteration while handling inputs, return, throw, and cleanup behavior.
misconceptions: Calling a generator function immediately runs its body to completion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use generators for lazy staged iteration while handling inputs, return, throw, and cleanup behavior.

competencies.statement: Create, read, update, delete, enumerate, and compute properties while distinguishing own and inherited state.
misconceptions: In and Object.hasOwn report exactly the same property set.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, read, update, delete, enumerate, and compute properties while distinguishing own and inherited state.

competencies.statement: Use object destructuring, rest, and spread while accounting for shallow copies, accessors, and overwrite order.
misconceptions: Object spread creates a deep clone of nested state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use object destructuring, rest, and spread while accounting for shallow copies, accessors, and overwrite order.

competencies.statement: Trace property lookup and method sharing through the prototype chain without class-only mental models.
misconceptions: Each instance stores a private copy of every prototype method.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace property lookup and method sharing through the prototype chain without class-only mental models.

competencies.statement: Use data or accessor descriptors and explain writable, enumerable, and configurable constraints.
misconceptions: Object.freeze recursively freezes every reachable object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use data or accessor descriptors and explain writable, enumerable, and configurable constraints.

competencies.statement: Apply preventExtensions, seal, freeze, or immutable update patterns at deliberate ownership boundaries.
misconceptions: Freezing every object is a complete state-management strategy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply preventExtensions, seal, freeze, or immutable update patterns at deliberate ownership boundaries.

competencies.statement: Validate external keys and use safe containers or allowlists to prevent prototype-pollution paths.
misconceptions: JSON data cannot affect object prototypes because it is only text.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate external keys and use safe containers or allowlists to prevent prototype-pollution paths.

competencies.statement: Explain classes as prototype-based constructs with strict bodies, constructors, methods, and fields.
misconceptions: JavaScript classes replace and bypass prototypes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain classes as prototype-based constructs with strict bodies, constructors, methods, and fields.

competencies.statement: Use private fields, static fields, and static blocks without confusing instance and constructor state.
misconceptions: A naming underscore creates language-enforced privacy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use private fields, static fields, and static blocks without confusing instance and constructor state.

competencies.statement: Use accessors for stable property semantics without hiding expensive work or surprising side effects.
misconceptions: A getter is equivalent to a cached public field.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use accessors for stable property semantics without hiding expensive work or surprising side effects.

competencies.statement: Use extends and super with correct construction and method lookup while avoiding fragile base-class contracts.
misconceptions: Inheritance is the safest default for sharing any behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use extends and super with correct construction and method lookup while avoiding fragile base-class contracts.

competencies.statement: Prefer composition and injected policies when behaviors vary independently or require isolated testing.
misconceptions: Composition means copying methods into every object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prefer composition and injected policies when behaviors vary independently or require isolated testing.

competencies.statement: Enforce constructor and method invariants with changed-case tests rather than relying on nominal class names.
misconceptions: Instanceof proves an object satisfies every behavioral requirement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce constructor and method invariants with changed-case tests rather than relying on nominal class names.

competencies.statement: Differentiate syntax, reference, type, range, aggregate, DOM, and domain errors by evidence and recovery boundary.
misconceptions: Every JavaScript failure is a generic Error with equivalent meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Differentiate syntax, reference, type, range, aggregate, DOM, and domain errors by evidence and recovery boundary.

competencies.statement: Throw Error objects with stable messages, structured context, and preserved causes without exposing secrets.
misconceptions: Throwing a string is equivalent to throwing an Error.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Throw Error objects with stable messages, structured context, and preserved causes without exposing secrets.

competencies.statement: Place try, catch, and finally around the smallest recoverable boundary without swallowing failures or overriding control flow.
misconceptions: A finally block runs only when catch handles an error.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Place try, catch, and finally around the smallest recoverable boundary without swallowing failures or overriding control flow.

competencies.statement: Create domain error types only when callers need stable programmatic distinctions and recovery actions.
misconceptions: Every unique message needs a unique error subclass.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create domain error types only when callers need stable programmatic distinctions and recovery actions.

competencies.statement: Reduce failures, form falsifiable hypotheses, inspect state and stacks, repair causes, and rerun regression cases.
misconceptions: The line that throws is always the line that created the defect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reduce failures, form falsifiable hypotheses, inspect state and stacks, repair causes, and rerun regression cases.

competencies.statement: Release listeners, timers, streams, locks, and disposable resources on success, failure, and cancellation.
misconceptions: Garbage collection automatically cancels all external resources.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Release listeners, timers, streams, locks, and disposable resources on success, failure, and cancellation.

competencies.statement: Trace call stack, task, microtask, rendering, and worker boundaries without treating timing as random.
misconceptions: Promise callbacks run immediately when a promise settles.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace call stack, task, microtask, rendering, and worker boundaries without treating timing as random.

competencies.statement: Create, return, chain, and observe promises while preserving values and rejection paths.
misconceptions: A fulfilled promise means the underlying user task succeeded.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, return, chain, and observe promises while preserving values and rejection paths.

competencies.statement: Use async and await as promise control flow without serializing independent work accidentally.
misconceptions: Await blocks the entire JavaScript runtime thread until completion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use async and await as promise control flow without serializing independent work accidentally.

competencies.statement: Choose all, allSettled, race, or any from failure, completion, and cancellation semantics.
misconceptions: Promise.race cancels every losing operation automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose all, allSettled, race, or any from failure, completion, and cancellation semantics.

competencies.statement: Propagate AbortSignal, deadlines, and cleanup through every cancellable asynchronous boundary.
misconceptions: Ignoring a late result is equivalent to cancelling its work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate AbortSignal, deadlines, and cleanup through every cancellable asynchronous boundary.

competencies.statement: Handle asynchronous failures at the owning boundary and prevent floating or unhandled promises.
misconceptions: A surrounding synchronous try-catch catches any later promise rejection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle asynchronous failures at the owning boundary and prevent floating or unhandled promises.

competencies.statement: Use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.
misconceptions: Default exports are required for one-value modules.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.

competencies.statement: Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.
misconceptions: Imports copy a snapshot of exported values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.

competencies.statement: Use dynamic import for conditional capability or measured loading needs with complete error states.
misconceptions: Dynamic import automatically improves performance in every case.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use dynamic import for conditional capability or measured loading needs with complete error states.

competencies.statement: Distinguish ESM and CommonJS resolution and interop without mixing assumptions across runtimes.
misconceptions: Require and import are interchangeable spellings.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish ESM and CommonJS resolution and interop without mixing assumptions across runtimes.

competencies.statement: Interpret package exports, imports, type, engines, scripts, lockfiles, and semantic versions at install and runtime boundaries.
misconceptions: A permissive version range guarantees compatible behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret package exports, imports, type, engines, scripts, lockfiles, and semantic versions at install and runtime boundaries.

competencies.statement: Minimize, verify, update, and audit dependencies while treating install scripts and transitive code as executable supply-chain input.
misconceptions: A popular package is automatically a safe dependency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Minimize, verify, update, and audit dependencies while treating install scripts and transitive code as executable supply-chain input.

competencies.statement: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.
misconceptions: Changing an HTML string is always safer than constructing DOM nodes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.

competencies.statement: Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
misconceptions: The DOM and an in-memory state object synchronize automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.

competencies.statement: Trace event target, currentTarget, capture, bubble, composed paths, and cancellation before delegating behavior.
misconceptions: StopPropagation also prevents the browser default action.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace event target, currentTarget, capture, bubble, composed paths, and cancellation before delegating behavior.

competencies.statement: Delegate stable event behavior using safe target narrowing and boundaries that survive dynamic content.
misconceptions: Every event bubbles through every DOM boundary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Delegate stable event behavior using safe target narrowing and boundaries that survive dynamic content.

competencies.statement: Preserve native keyboard behavior, visible focus, logical focus movement, and restoration after dynamic changes.
misconceptions: Click handlers automatically provide complete keyboard interaction.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve native keyboard behavior, visible focus, logical focus movement, and restoration after dynamic changes.

competencies.statement: Announce meaningful asynchronous changes without noisy live regions or focus theft.
misconceptions: Moving focus to every updated message is the most accessible announcement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Announce meaningful asynchronous changes without noisy live regions or focus theft.

competencies.statement: Use fetch with explicit method, headers, body, status checks, parsing, abort, and failure-state behavior.
misconceptions: Fetch rejects its promise for every HTTP error status.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use fetch with explicit method, headers, body, status checks, parsing, abort, and failure-state behavior.

competencies.statement: Construct and parse URL, URLSearchParams, FormData, and encoded payloads without manual delimiter bugs.
misconceptions: String concatenation is sufficient for every URL and form body.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct and parse URL, URLSearchParams, FormData, and encoded payloads without manual delimiter bugs.

competencies.statement: Parse, validate, transform, and serialize JSON while accounting for unsupported values and hostile object shape.
misconceptions: Successful JSON.parse proves the data satisfies the application schema.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse, validate, transform, and serialize JSON while accounting for unsupported values and hostile object shape.

competencies.statement: Choose memory, sessionStorage, localStorage, Cache, or IndexedDB from size, lifetime, concurrency, privacy, and transaction needs.
misconceptions: LocalStorage is a secure database for secrets and user records.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose memory, sessionStorage, localStorage, Cache, or IndexedDB from size, lifetime, concurrency, privacy, and transaction needs.

competencies.statement: Keep untrusted data in text or validated structured sinks and avoid dynamic code or unsafe markup construction.
misconceptions: Escaping angle brackets once makes a value safe in every browser context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep untrusted data in text or validated structured sinks and avoid dynamic code or unsafe markup construction.

competencies.statement: Explain origin, CORS, CSP, credentials, workers, and permission prompts as layered browser boundaries.
misconceptions: CORS is an authentication system that protects server data from all clients.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain origin, CORS, CSP, credentials, workers, and permission prompts as layered browser boundaries.

competencies.statement: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
misconceptions: High line coverage proves the important behavior is correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.

competencies.statement: Use fakes, stubs, spies, and controlled clocks only at owned boundaries without mocking away the behavior under test.
misconceptions: Every dependency should be mocked in a unit test.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use fakes, stubs, spies, and controlled clocks only at owned boundaries without mocking away the behavior under test.

competencies.statement: Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.
misconceptions: Adding a delay is a reliable way to test asynchronous completion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.

competencies.statement: Enforce formatting, linting, type-aware JavaScript checks, tests, dead-code detection, and dependency audit in local and CI gates.
misconceptions: A passing linter proves runtime behavior and domain correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce formatting, linting, type-aware JavaScript checks, tests, dead-code detection, and dependency audit in local and CI gates.

competencies.statement: Measure startup, long tasks, memory retention, network, rendering, and algorithm cost before optimizing a bottleneck.
misconceptions: Shorter source code is necessarily faster JavaScript.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure startup, long tasks, memory retention, network, rendering, and algorithm cost before optimizing a bottleneck.

competencies.statement: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
misconceptions: A successful local run is sufficient release proof.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.

credential.title: Modern JavaScript: Runtime to Accessible Application Certificate

modules.title: JavaScript Runtime, Tooling, and Evidence
modules.description: Trace a small service-status script from source through parsing, execution, console evidence, and a user-visible result. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible service-status runtime notebook through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish ECMAScript language behavior from browser, worker, and server host APIs.
Trace source parsing, module instantiation, execution contexts, jobs, and observable output.
Use console evidence, breakpoints, scope inspection, watch expressions, and stack traces without relying on logging alone.

modules.title: Values, Types, Coercion, and Equality
modules.description: Normalize prices, quantities, identifiers, optional fields, and validity signals from a mixed service-data feed. Learners cumulatively produce a value-normalization and validation pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish undefined, null, Boolean, Number, BigInt, String, Symbol, and object values by behavior and use.
Use typeof, Array.isArray, instanceof, and explicit predicates while accounting for their boundaries.
Predict implicit coercion and prefer explicit conversion at external data boundaries.

modules.title: Bindings, Scope, Closures, and Lifetime
modules.description: Build configurable counters and validators without leaking mutable state across community-service widgets. Learners cumulatively produce a closure-backed state and validation library through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose const, let, or legacy var from reassignment, scope, and initialization behavior.
Resolve names through lexical environments and identify shadowing without guessing from call order.
Predict declaration instantiation, function hoisting, and temporal dead-zone failures.

modules.title: Expressions, Decisions, and Iteration
modules.description: Route support cases through explicit rules and iterate changing record sets without truthiness or termination defects. Learners cumulatively produce a transparent support-case routing engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Predict expression grouping, short-circuit evaluation, assignment, optional chaining, and nullish coalescing.
Use truthiness only when every falsy value shares the same domain meaning and otherwise write explicit guards.
Express mutually exclusive branches, guard clauses, and exhaustive fallbacks without unreachable paths.

modules.title: Functions, Parameters, this, and Callable Contracts
modules.description: Refactor the routing engine into testable transformations, callbacks, methods, and boundary adapters. Learners cumulatively produce a function-oriented routing toolkit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose function declarations, expressions, arrows, and methods from hoisting, this, construction, and readability needs.
Use defaults, rest parameters, destructuring, and validation without hiding required inputs.
Separate returned values, mutations, I/O, and thrown failures into an understandable callable contract.

modules.title: Arrays, Collections, Iterables, and Generators
modules.description: Transform ordered requests, unique identifiers, lookup tables, and streaming batches while preserving ownership and order. Learners cumulatively produce a collection-processing and streaming pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose mutating or copying array operations and document ownership before updating shared data.
Use map, filter, reduce, find, some, every, flat, and flatMap from the required output and early-exit behavior.
Sort with a valid comparator, copying policy, stability expectations, and locale-aware text rules.

modules.title: Objects, Prototypes, Properties, and Data Shape
modules.description: Model service records and shared behavior while defending against accidental mutation and unsafe property input. Learners cumulatively produce a validated service-record domain model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create, read, update, delete, enumerate, and compute properties while distinguishing own and inherited state.
Use object destructuring, rest, and spread while accounting for shallow copies, accessors, and overwrite order.
Trace property lookup and method sharing through the prototype chain without class-only mental models.

modules.title: Classes, Private State, Inheritance, and Composition
modules.description: Build maintainable scheduling components with explicit invariants and replaceable policies. Learners cumulatively produce a policy-driven scheduling component library through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain classes as prototype-based constructs with strict bodies, constructors, methods, and fields.
Use private fields, static fields, and static blocks without confusing instance and constructor state.
Use accessors for stable property semantics without hiding expensive work or surprising side effects.

modules.title: Errors, Cleanup, and Root-Cause Debugging
modules.description: Diagnose malformed records and failed operations while keeping cleanup, user recovery, and causal evidence intact. Learners cumulatively produce a recoverable import and diagnostics workflow through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Differentiate syntax, reference, type, range, aggregate, DOM, and domain errors by evidence and recovery boundary.
Throw Error objects with stable messages, structured context, and preserved causes without exposing secrets.
Place try, catch, and finally around the smallest recoverable boundary without swallowing failures or overriding control flow.

modules.title: Promises, Async Functions, and the Event Loop
modules.description: Coordinate status requests, deadlines, retries, and user cancellation without races or unhandled failures. Learners cumulatively produce a bounded asynchronous status coordinator through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace call stack, task, microtask, rendering, and worker boundaries without treating timing as random.
Create, return, chain, and observe promises while preserving values and rejection paths.
Use async and await as promise control flow without serializing independent work accidentally.

modules.title: Modules, Packages, and Dependency Boundaries
modules.description: Split the status coordinator into explicit browser modules and verify clean-environment loading and dependency behavior. Learners cumulatively produce a modular status application with a dependency record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.
Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.
Use dynamic import for conditional capability or measured loading needs with complete error states.

modules.title: DOM State, Events, and Accessible Interaction
modules.description: Build a keyboard-operable service-status interface whose DOM, focus, announcements, and visual state stay synchronized. Learners cumulatively produce an accessible interactive service-status interface through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.
Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
Trace event target, currentTarget, capture, bubble, composed paths, and cancellation before delegating behavior.

modules.title: Web Data, Fetch, Storage, and Security Boundaries
modules.description: Load and cache public service data while handling hostile input, offline states, privacy, and response failures. Learners cumulatively produce a resilient privacy-aware service-data client through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use fetch with explicit method, headers, body, status checks, parsing, abort, and failure-state behavior.
Construct and parse URL, URLSearchParams, FormData, and encoded payloads without manual delimiter bugs.
Parse, validate, transform, and serialize JSON while accounting for unsupported values and hostile object shape.

modules.title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release
modules.description: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Learners cumulatively produce a tested and release-ready JavaScript application through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
Use fakes, stubs, spies, and controlled clocks only at owned boundaries without mocking away the behavior under test.
Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.
